title: Wet Mediterranean Yellow Split Peas Stew
description: A bitter, gooey stewed dish of yellow split peas and lardons, simmered in a bamboo steamer for a monsoon‑inspired Mediterranean flavor.
instructions: Rinse the yellow split peas under cold water until clear.
In a large saucepan, bring the 4 cups of water to a boil, then add the peas and reduce to a simmer for 15 minutes.
While the peas cook, heat a skillet over medium heat and render the lardons until crispy, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add a tablespoon of olive oil and sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent.
Add the diced tomatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes until they soften.
Stir in the cumin and coriander powders, cooking for 1 minute to release their aromas.
Pour the tomato mixture into the saucepan with the peas, and add the chopped kale.
Season the stew with salt and pepper, then cover and steam in a bamboo steamer for 20 minutes.
After steaming, stir in the rendered lardons and adjust seasoning if needed.
Transfer the stew to serving bowls, letting the residual heat make it gooey and silky.
Optional: squeeze a splash of lemon juice for brightness before serving.
Serve hot as a nourishing Mediterranean lunch.
